_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse how representations of people, places, events and concepts in texts reflect the contexts in which those texts were created.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse how representations of people, places, events and concepts in texts reflect the contexts in which those texts were created.
y: All representations are constructed and contextual — understanding this is fundamental to critical literacy.
This analytical skill helps us evaluate the texts we encounter with sophistication and discernment.
Recognising how context shapes representation is essential for informed, ethical reading.
eg: We analyse how a text's representation of a community reflects the author's cultural position and the text's historical context.
We compare representations of the same concept in texts from different time periods and contexts.
We evaluate how media representations of events are shaped by the outlet's editorial context.
We discuss how our own context influences the representations we create in our writing.
We identify how texts can naturalise particular representations, making constructed views seem like common sense.
We write an analysis of how representation in a specific text reflects its context.
